title: Countertop Pie Safe
summary: A small countertop cabinet with an acrylic-glass door — see what's inside without opening it, and keep flies off a cooling pie at the same time.
description:
This is a genuinely small build — a 1x12 box with a shelf, sized to sit on a counter rather than the floor, held square with 1x2 top supports before the plywood back goes on. The 1x4 top pieces and their 1x2 cap trim overhang slightly, giving the little cabinet a finished cap rather than a flush plywood edge.

**The door is a square-dowel frame around a piece of acrylic glass, not a solid panel** — that's the entire point of a pie safe: you can see what's cooling inside without opening the door and losing the heat, or letting flies in. Build the dowel frame to hold the acrylic pane, then hang the whole door on small hinges.

A knob or handle finishes the door, sized to match the piece — anything oversized will look wrong on a cabinet this small.

tools.note: For fitting the acrylic pane opening in the door frame.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood scrap, at least 12 in x 9 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood scrap, at least 12 in x 9 in

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut three sides and bottom at 11-1/4" and one shelf at 10" from the 1x12. Cut two top supports at 11-1/4" and one top cap trim at 13-3/4" from the 1x2. Cut one back at 12-3/4" from the 1/4" plywood scrap, at least 12 in x 9 in. Cut three top boards at 13-3/4" from the 1x4. Cut two door frame, sides at 11" and two door frame, top/bottom at 6-1/2" from the 3/4" square dowel. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to one 4 ft 1x12, one 4 ft 1x2 and one 4 ft 1x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.
materials: 1/4" plywood scrap, at least 12 in x 9 in

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Assemble the sides, bottom, and shelf into a small box with the top supports keeping it square.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Nail the plywood back panel to the box.
materials: 1/4" plywood scrap, at least 12 in x 9 in

steps.title: Add the top
steps.body: Attach the top boards and cap them with the 1x2 top cap trim, overhanging slightly.

steps.title: Build and hang the door
steps.body: Build the square-dowel frame around the acrylic pane, then hang the door with small hinges.

steps.title: Add the knob and finish
steps.body: Attach the knob or handle, then sand and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Small countertop pie safe with acrylic glass door
